channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
item.description: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

item.title: Building a Kubernetes Cluster on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10: A kubeadm Guide
item.description: Introduction In this post, I'll walk you through deploying a production-ready Kubernetes cluster on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 using kubeadm. This lab was inspired by Anthony E. Nocentino's...

item.title: Swift VSX Support, Biome Type Inference, Agent Guardrails
item.description: This week's tooling news clusters around a recurring theme: removing dependencies that were never really necessary. Biome ditches the TypeScript compiler for type-aware linting. Swift developers...

item.title: AI Can Write the Code. Who Gives It the Context?
item.description: When you talk to ChatGPT about a subject you understand well, you quickly notice something. The first answer is rarely the final answer. You add context. You correct an assumption. You explain...

item.title: The Cheaper API Was 2.5x Cheaper. It Cost 1.6x More.
item.description: AI-disclosure: AI-assisted draft, human-reviewed. The demo numbers are the verbatim stdout of a deterministic, stdlib-only Python script included in full below — re-run it and you get the same...

item.title: Workflow SDK AbortController + Claude Fable 5: Issue #38
item.description: This week's AI tooling news splits cleanly between infrastructure you can ship today and capability bets that require more careful evaluation. Anthropic dropped two significant releases—Fable 5...

item.title: Hyperpb Parser Matches Generated Code Speed
item.description: This week's tooling news splits cleanly between performance and compliance: a Go Protobuf parser that closes the gap between reflection and generated code, and a GitLab update that finally makes...

item.title: IDE fixes, TS 5.9 beta, Claude tool use explained
item.description: This week landed a mix of maintenance you can't skip and concepts worth understanding before they bite you in production. The Continue plugin fixes address real crash vectors that have been...

item.title: Biome v1.7 + 5 dev tool updates this week
item.description: This week's tooling landscape is defined by a recurring theme: reducing operational overhead at the architecture level rather than patching symptoms. Biome automates away the ESLint migration...

item.title: Ruff 0.6 stabilizes notebook linting, new SDKs ship
item.description: This week's releases split neatly into two themes: Python tooling getting quieter (Ruff shipping defaults that just work) and the multimodal model landscape getting louder (NVIDIA and Microsoft...

item.title: Swift 1.0 Stable APIs + Rust 1.95 Language Features
item.description: This week's releases are less about flashy new capabilities and more about the quieter, more valuable kind of progress: stable APIs that let you stop maintaining workarounds. Swift's server...

item.title: MosaicLeaks: Can your research agent keep a secret?
This robotic self-driving toilet comes to you
item.description: During a recent expo in Shanghai that focuses on elderly care, assistive devices, and rehabilitation medicine, a Chinese company called Yueban debuted a smart toilet that does something we haven't...

item.title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
item.description: FERC told grid operators to give data centers a fast lane for interconnections, but it failed to address electricity supply shortages.

item.title: Before SpaceX IPO, investors in China secretly acquired stakes
item.description: One previously unreported SpaceX investor has ties to Chinese military contractors.

item.title: ‘Popa’ Botnet Linked to Publicly-Traded Israeli Firm
item.description: For the past four years, a sprawling Android-based botnet called Popa has forced millions of consumer TV boxes to relay Internet traffic linked to advertising fraud, account takeovers, and mass...

item.title: A year of Walmart Plus is half off ahead of Prime Day
item.description: Do you often find yourself shopping at the big blue, or perhaps you’re just looking for an alternative to Amazon? Either way, Walmart is currently offering a year of its Walmart Plus subscription...

item.title: Firefox’s new home page widgets are helping me focus
item.description: I launched Firefox this morning to find some new blocks on my home page. The widgets that are currently rolling out add sports scores, time zones, a focus timer, and a checklist, which are already...

item.title: The smartphone era created an attention crisis. Slowtech is fixing it
item.description: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

item.title: The Onion’s rebooted InfoWars is coming July 2nd
item.description: The Onion's InfoWars officially has a launch date: On July 2nd, the conspiracy network previously run by Alex Jones will return as a comedy and media platform. The reboot comes more than a year...

item.title: ‘Queer Eye’s’ life coach Karamo Brown launches Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone
item.description: Karamo Brown, famous for his pep talks on Netflix’s “Queer Eye,” has jumped into the wellness and AI space with his new app, Kē. After spending a year and a half focusing on his own journey—from...

item.title: 3 Amazon Workers Say They’re Under Investigation for Speaking Out About Data Centers
item.description: The software engineers filed a complaint with Seattle’s civil rights office accusing Amazon of illegally retaliating against them for expressing their personal political beliefs.

item.title: Amazon employees say they’re facing termination for backing data center limits
item.description: When three Amazon software engineers testified earlier this month at Seattle City Council hearings about data centers, they started their testimony by citing a city law barring employment...

item.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
item.description: If an autonomous AI agent interacts with your company's core intellectual property today, can your security team instantly name the person who authorized it? For most enterprises, the answer is a...

item.title: No more lightbulbs, much more sports: Five predictions for Roku’s future
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. When Fox announced its...

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Claude Chat Abuse, NastyC2 npm Packages, Device-Code Phishing + 25 More Stories
item.description: The internet did not break this week. It got used exactly as designed, which is worse. Searches were siphoned through shady browser add-ons. AI chat links turned into malware delivery paths. macOS...

item.title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
item.description: The startup trains embodied AI and world models using Medal’s dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million monthly active users.

item.title: A tech worker-backed PAC is bringing a $5M knife to Big Tech’s $100M gunfight
item.description: Guardrails positions itself as a populist political movement that runs on small donations from people in the trenches of the AI boom.

item.title: Google Calendar finally has more color options for events
item.description: Running out of color options for events in Google Calendar shouldn't be an issue going forward. The previous limit of 11 predefined colors has now been expanded to give users access to up to 200...

item.title: Microsoft Details Windows Clipper Malware Campaign Using USB LNK Worm and Tor-Based C2
item.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a Windows-based cryptocurrency clipper campaign that has targeted users since February 2026. "The clipper in this campaign relies on Windows Script Host and...
